rights.text: Security deposit must be returned within 35 days of move-out with an itemised list of deductions.
Landlord must maintain the premises in a safe, habitable condition - implied warranty applies.
Landlord must give reasonable advance notice before entering - courts typically apply a 24-hour standard.
Tenant may terminate for uninhabitable conditions after giving landlord 14 days' written notice to remedy.
To end a month-to-month tenancy, either party must give 30 days' notice.
A landlord may charge a security deposit of no more than 1 month's rent (excluding certain pet and risk deposits).

State law requires landlords to give tenants specific written disclosures (for example, the owner or manager's identity, how the security deposit is held, shared-utility arrangements, and known hazards).
A landlord cannot retaliate against a tenant (for example by raising rent, cutting services, or evicting) for complaining about conditions or exercising a legal right.
Eviction for unpaid rent: the tenant has 7 business days to pay or move before the landlord can file.
If the landlord fails to make major repairs, the tenant may withhold rent or repair and deduct the cost under state law.

programs.name: AHFA Section 8 Housing Choice Vouchers
programs.who: Low-income renters, families, seniors, disabled
programs.what: Subsidises rent so you pay no more than 30% of your income. Vouchers are portable across Alabama.

programs.name: Emergency Rental Assistance (ERA)
programs.who: Renters facing eviction or utility shutoff due to hardship
programs.what: Pays up to 12–18 months of overdue rent and utilities directly to landlords and utility companies.

programs.name: HOME Investment Partnerships Program
programs.org: Alabama Department of Economic & Community Affairs
programs.who: Low-income households, first-time renters, families
programs.what: Funds affordable rental housing construction and tenant-based rental assistance statewide.

programs.name: USDA Section 515 Rural Rental Housing
programs.who: Very low-income rural renters, seniors, disabled
programs.what: Below-market rent in USDA-financed rural apartment communities across Alabama.

programs.name: Alabama's Low Income Home Energy Assistance (LIHEAP)
programs.who: Low-income renters struggling with utility costs
programs.what: Covers heating and cooling costs - freeing up income for rent. Often paired with ERA funding.

rights.text: Security deposit must be returned within 14 days (or 30 days if deductions are itemised) of move-out.
Landlord must give 24 hours' notice before entering for non-emergency repairs or inspections.
Landlord must keep the premises in habitable condition and make necessary repairs in a reasonable time.
Tenant may repair and deduct repair costs from rent for urgent issues after proper written notice.
To end a month-to-month tenancy, either party must give 30 days' notice.
A landlord may charge a security deposit of no more than 2 months' rent (unless rent exceeds $2,000/month).

State law requires landlords to give tenants specific written disclosures (for example, the owner or manager's identity, how the security deposit is held, shared-utility arrangements, and known hazards).
A landlord cannot retaliate against a tenant (for example by raising rent, cutting services, or evicting) for complaining about conditions or exercising a legal right.
Eviction for unpaid rent: the tenant has 7 days to pay or move.
If the landlord fails to make major repairs, the tenant may withhold rent or repair and deduct the cost under state law.

programs.name: AHFC Rental Housing Programs
programs.who: Low-to-moderate income Alaskans, seniors, veterans
programs.what: Section 8 vouchers, public housing, and workforce housing loans. AHFC is Alaska's primary affordable housing agency.

programs.name: Weatherization & Cold Climate Housing Program
programs.who: Low-income Alaskan renters in cold rural areas
programs.what: Free energy efficiency upgrades to rental units - saving renters hundreds per year on heating costs.

programs.name: Alaska Senior Housing Initiative
programs.who: Alaskans 60+, especially those in remote villages
programs.what: Subsidised senior housing developments and home modification grants to help seniors age in place.

programs.name: Alaska Native Tribal Housing
programs.who: Alaska Native and American Indian tribal members
programs.what: Tribally administered rental housing under NAHASDA - often the most affordable option in village communities.

programs.name: AHFC Veterans Mortgage Program
programs.who: Alaskan veterans and active duty military
programs.what: Below-market mortgage rates for veterans; paired with rental bridge assistance for transitioning servicemembers.

rights.text: Security deposit must be returned within 14 days of move-out with an itemised deduction statement.
Landlord must give at least 2 days' notice before entering for non-emergency access.
Landlord must maintain fit and habitable premises and make repairs within 10 days of written notice.
Tenant may terminate the lease for uninhabitable conditions after 5 days' notice if landlord fails to act.
To end a month-to-month tenancy, either party must give 30 days' notice.
A landlord may charge a security deposit of no more than 1.5 months' rent.
Late fees must be stated in a written agreement and must be reasonable.

State law requires landlords to give tenants specific written disclosures (for example, the owner or manager's identity, how the security deposit is held, shared-utility arrangements, and known hazards).

A landlord cannot retaliate against a tenant (for example by raising rent, cutting services, or evicting) for complaining about conditions or exercising a legal right. Retaliation is presumed if the landlord takes a negative action within 6 months of the tenant's protected act.
Eviction for unpaid rent: the tenant has 5 days to pay or move.
If the landlord fails to make major repairs, the tenant may withhold rent or repair and deduct the cost under state law.

programs.name: Arizona Department of Housing Rental Assistance
programs.who: Low-income Arizona renters, families with children
programs.what: Administers Section 8 vouchers and HOME-funded rental assistance. Over 30,000 Arizona households served.

programs.name: Arizona Tribal HUD-VASH
programs.who: Native American veterans experiencing homelessness
programs.what: Combines VA healthcare with HUD rental vouchers specifically designed for Native veterans on or near tribal lands.

programs.name: School of Choice Housing (Affordable Near Schools)
programs.who: Families with school-age children near high-quality public schools
programs.what: Income-qualifying families receive priority placement in subsidised units near top-rated school districts.

programs.name: Area Agency on Aging – Senior Rental Assistance
programs.who: Seniors 60+ in Maricopa County
programs.what: Emergency rent and utility assistance for seniors at risk of homelessness; caseworker support included.

programs.name: Arizona SNAP Employment & Training Housing Bridge
programs.who: SNAP recipients entering workforce training programs
programs.what: Short-term rental assistance while participants complete job training; priority for single parents.

rights.text: Security deposit must be returned within 60 days of lease termination.
Landlord must maintain the unit in a safe, habitable condition under implied warranty principles.
Landlord must provide reasonable advance notice before entering - no specific hours set by statute.
Tenant may withhold rent for habitability failures after providing the landlord written notice and opportunity to repair.
To end a month-to-month tenancy, either party must give 30 days' notice.
A landlord may charge a security deposit of no more than 2 months' rent (limit excludes landlords owning 5 or fewer units who self-manage).

State law requires landlords to give tenants specific written disclosures (for example, the owner or manager's identity, how the security deposit is held, shared-utility arrangements, and known hazards).
A landlord cannot retaliate against a tenant (for example by raising rent, cutting services, or evicting) for complaining about conditions or exercising a legal right.
Eviction for unpaid rent: the tenant has 3 days to move; Arkansas uses unconditional quit notices, with no automatic chance to pay and stay.
Arkansas does not allow tenants to withhold rent or repair and deduct for habitability problems.

programs.name: ADFA Rental Assistance Programme
programs.who: Low-income Arkansas renters, households below 80% AMI
programs.what: Emergency rental assistance and LIHTC-funded affordable units. Apply through local Community Action Agencies.

programs.name: Delta Regional Authority Housing Assistance
programs.who: Rural and Delta-region residents, predominantly Black communities historically underserved
programs.what: Targeted affordable housing funds for the Arkansas Delta - one of America's most housing-stressed regions.

programs.name: Arkansas Senior Living Network
programs.org: Arkansas Division of Aging & Adult Services
programs.who: Arkansans 62+, low income
programs.what: Connects seniors to subsidised senior apartments, Section 202 housing, and rent reduction programs.

programs.name: Veterans & Military Families Housing Grant
programs.who: Arkansas veterans, especially those in rural areas
programs.what: One-time housing stabilisation grants for veterans facing eviction or homelessness; pairs with VA case management.

programs.name: USDA 502 Direct Loan & Rental Assistance
programs.who: Very low-income rural Arkansas households
programs.what: Below-market rural rental units and direct loans. Approximately 40% of Arkansas is USDA-eligible rural territory.

rights.text: Security deposit capped at 2 months' rent for unfurnished units; 3 months for furnished.
Landlord must give 24 hours' written notice before non-emergency entry.
Deposit must be returned within 21 days of move-out with an itemised statement.
Annual rent increases capped at 5% + local CPI (max 10%) in buildings 15+ years old.
To end a month-to-month tenancy, the landlord must give 30 days' notice if the tenant has lived there under 12 months; at 12 months or more, just cause is required.
Since July 2024, most landlords may charge no more than 1 month's rent as a deposit; small landlords (individuals owning up to two properties / four units) may charge up to 2 months.
A late fee must be a reasonable estimate of the landlord's actual damages; preset penalty fees may be unenforceable.
rights.citation: Orozco v. Casimiro, 121 Cal. App. 4th Supp. 7 (2004)

rights.text: State law requires landlords to give tenants specific written disclosures (for example, the owner or manager's identity, how the security deposit is held, shared-utility arrangements, and known hazards).

A landlord cannot retaliate against a tenant (for example by raising rent, cutting services, or evicting) for complaining about conditions or exercising a legal right. Retaliation is presumed if the landlord takes a negative action within 180 days of the tenant's protected act.
Eviction for unpaid rent: the tenant has 3 days (excluding weekends and court holidays) to pay or move.
If the landlord fails to make major repairs, the tenant may withhold rent or repair and deduct the cost under state law.
rights.citation: Cal. Civ. Code § 1942; Cal. Civ. Proc. Code § 1174.2

programs.name: CalHFA MyHome Assistance Programme
programs.who: First-time homebuyers earning under income limits; priority for renters transitioning to ownership
programs.what: Deferred-payment second loan of up to 3.5% of purchase price for down payment. No interest while you live in the home.

programs.name: Housing Choice Voucher / Section 8 (HACLA, SHRA, etc.)
programs.who: Very low-income households (below 50% AMI), seniors, disabled, large families
programs.what: Subsidises rent so tenant pays 30% of income. Waiting lists can be long - apply to multiple counties.

programs.name: California Dream for All Shared Appreciation Loan
programs.who: First-generation homebuyers, lower-income Californians
programs.what: State pays 20% of home purchase price interest-free; you repay when you sell - designed to address generational wealth gaps.

programs.name: Senior Citizen Rent Stabilisation Exemptions
programs.who: Low-income renters 62+ or disabled in rent-controlled units
programs.what: Freezes rent for qualifying seniors and disabled tenants. Los Angeles SCRIE program is one of the strongest in the country.

programs.name: LGBTQ+ Housing Assistance (SF, LA, SD programs)
programs.org: SF Human Rights Commission, LA LGBT Center, etc.
programs.who: LGBTQ+ renters, youth, those fleeing family rejection
programs.what: Emergency housing, transitional housing, and rental assistance specifically designed for LGBTQ+ individuals and families.

programs.name: UC/CSU Student Emergency Housing Funds
programs.org: University of California, Cal State systems
programs.who: Enrolled students experiencing housing insecurity
programs.what: Emergency micro-grants for rent, deposits, and short-term housing. Most campuses have a Basic Needs Centre - apply there.

programs.name: California Veterans Housing & Homelessness Prevention
programs.who: California veterans, homeless or at risk
programs.what: Rental assistance, transitional housing, and rapid rehousing - plus case management through the VA system.

rights.text: Security deposit must be returned within 30 days of move-out (60 days if lease specifies).
Landlord must give 24 hours' notice before entering for non-emergency maintenance or inspection.
Landlord must maintain a warranty of habitability - tenant may terminate if landlord fails to remedy.
Tenant may withhold rent or repair and deduct if landlord fails to address habitability issues within notice period.
To end a month-to-month tenancy, either party must give 21 days' notice.
A landlord may charge a security deposit of no more than 2 months' rent.
A late fee may be charged only after rent is at least 7 days late, and cannot exceed the greater of $50 or 5% of the past-due rent.

State law requires landlords to give tenants specific written disclosures (for example, the owner or manager's identity, how the security deposit is held, shared-utility arrangements, and known hazards).
A landlord cannot retaliate against a tenant (for example by raising rent, cutting services, or evicting) for complaining about conditions or exercising a legal right.
Eviction for unpaid rent: the tenant has 10 days to pay or move (5 days for certain single-family rentals).
If the landlord fails to make major repairs, the tenant may withhold rent or repair and deduct the cost under state law.

programs.name: CHFA Affordable Rental Housing
programs.who: Low-to-moderate income Colorado renters and homebuyers
programs.what: CHFA finances affordable apartments statewide; also offers down payment assistance for first-time buyers. Search CHFA's property map.

programs.name: Colorado Emergency Rental Assistance Programme (ERAP)
programs.who: Renters at risk of eviction, households behind on rent
programs.what: Covers past-due rent, prospective rent, and utilities. Apply through your county's Department of Human Services.

programs.name: Colorado Affordable Residences for Students (CARS)
programs.who: College students in affordable rental housing near campus
programs.what: Income-qualifying students receive access to below-market apartments near Colorado universities and community colleges.

programs.name: Section 202 Supportive Housing for the Elderly – Colorado
programs.who: Very low-income Coloradans 62+
programs.what: Subsidised apartment complexes with supportive services; many include transportation, meals, and social programming.

programs.name: Colorado Division of Housing Native American Housing
programs.who: Native American residents of Colorado's tribal communities
programs.what: Funding for tribally-administered affordable housing on and near Colorado reservations.

rights.text: Security deposit must be returned within 30 days (15 days if no deductions are claimed).
Deposit capped at 2 months' rent (1 month's rent for tenants 62 and older).
Landlord must give reasonable notice - generally interpreted as 24 hours - before entering.
Landlord must keep premises in a habitable condition; tenant may withhold rent for serious violations.
A landlord must give 3 days' notice to terminate a month-to-month tenancy.
A landlord may charge no more than 2 months' rent as a deposit (1 month if the tenant is 62 or older).
A late fee cannot be charged until rent is 9 days late (4 days for weekly tenancies), capped at $5/day up to $50, or 5%, whichever is greater.

State law requires landlords to give tenants specific written disclosures (for example, the owner or manager's identity, how the security deposit is held, shared-utility arrangements, and known hazards).

A landlord cannot retaliate against a tenant (for example by raising rent, cutting services, or evicting) for complaining about conditions or exercising a legal right. Retaliation is presumed if the landlord takes a negative action within 6 months of the tenant's protected act.
Eviction for unpaid rent: the tenant has 9 days to pay or move.
If the landlord fails to make major repairs, the tenant may withhold rent or repair and deduct the cost under state law.

programs.name: CHFA Rental Assistance Programme
programs.who: Low-income Connecticut renters, families, elderly
programs.what: Administers Section 8 vouchers and finances affordable rental properties; partners with local housing authorities.

programs.name: UniteCT Emergency Rental Assistance
programs.who: CT renters behind on rent due to COVID or economic hardship
programs.what: Up to 18 months of rent and utility assistance paid directly to landlords. Apply online at UniteCT.com.

programs.name: ConneCT Opportunity Youth Housing
programs.who: Youth ages 16–24 who are homeless or aging out of foster care
programs.what: Rapid rehousing, transitional housing, and host home programs specifically designed for young people.

programs.name: Connecticut Elderly Renter Tax Credit
programs.who: Renters 65+ or 50+ if surviving spouse of elderly renter
programs.what: Annual tax credit of up to $900 to offset rental costs - apply on your state income tax return.

programs.name: African American Affairs Commission Housing Programme
programs.who: Black Connecticut residents facing housing discrimination or hardship
programs.what: Connects residents to fair housing legal aid, emergency funds, and affordable housing waitlists statewide.

rights.text: Security deposit must be returned within 20 days of move-out with an itemised deduction statement.
Landlord must give 24 hours' notice before entering except in emergencies.
Landlord must maintain the dwelling in a safe and habitable condition throughout the tenancy.
Deposit capped at 1 month's rent for tenancies month-to-month; 2 months for longer leases.
To end a month-to-month tenancy, either party must give 60 days' notice.
On leases of one year or more, a landlord may charge no more than 1 month's rent as a deposit.
A late fee cannot exceed 5% of rent and cannot be imposed until rent is more than 5 days late.

State law requires landlords to give tenants specific written disclosures (for example, the owner or manager's identity, how the security deposit is held, shared-utility arrangements, and known hazards).

A landlord cannot retaliate against a tenant (for example by raising rent, cutting services, or evicting) for complaining about conditions or exercising a legal right. Retaliation is presumed if the landlord takes a negative action within 90 days of the tenant's protected act.
Eviction for unpaid rent: the tenant has 5 days to pay or move.
If the landlord fails to make major repairs, the tenant may withhold rent or repair and deduct the cost under state law.

programs.name: DSHA Section 8 / Housing Choice Vouchers
programs.who: Very low-income Delawareans, including working families and seniors
programs.what: Subsidy that lets you rent a private unit while paying only 30% of income. Apply at DSHA.Delaware.gov.

programs.name: Delaware Emergency Rental Assistance Programme
programs.who: Renters impacted by income loss or pandemic-related hardship
programs.what: Pays overdue rent and utilities directly to landlords and utility providers on the tenant's behalf.

programs.name: Delaware Hispanic Community Housing Initiative
programs.who: Delaware's Hispanic and Latino renter community
programs.what: Bilingual housing counselling, fair housing assistance, and connection to affordable rentals and homeownership programs.

programs.name: Home Modification Loan Programme – Renters
programs.who: Delaware renters with disabilities
programs.what: Zero-interest loans for accessibility modifications (ramps, grab bars, widened doorways) in rented homes with landlord approval.

programs.name: Delaware Farmworker Housing Grant
programs.who: Seasonal and migrant farmworkers and their families
programs.what: Safe, affordable housing near agricultural worksites in rural Delaware; often Spanish-speaking staff available.

rights.text: Deposit must be returned within 15 days (or 30 days if deductions are claimed) of move-out.
Landlord must give at least 12 hours' notice before non-emergency entry.
Landlord must maintain premises in a fit and habitable condition throughout the tenancy.
Tenant may withhold rent if landlord fails to repair material defects after 7-day written notice.
To end a month-to-month tenancy, either party must give 30 days' notice.
Florida sets no cap on the deposit amount, but statute governs how deposits must be held and returned.

State law requires landlords to give tenants specific written disclosures (for example, the owner or manager's identity, how the security deposit is held, shared-utility arrangements, and known hazards).
A landlord cannot retaliate against a tenant (for example by raising rent, cutting services, or evicting) for complaining about conditions or exercising a legal right.
Eviction for unpaid rent: the tenant has 3 days (excluding weekends and holidays) to pay or move.
If the landlord fails to make major repairs, the tenant may withhold rent or repair and deduct the cost under state law.

programs.name: Florida Housing Finance Corporation – Rental Assistance
programs.who: Low-income Florida renters, large families, seniors, farmworkers
programs.what: Administers LIHTC affordable apartments and Section 8 vouchers statewide; search FloridaHousing.org for available units.

programs.name: SHIP (State Housing Initiatives Partnership)
programs.who: Very low, low, and moderate income Floridians
programs.what: Funded through doc stamp taxes - provides down payment assistance, rental subsidies, and repair grants through counties.

programs.name: Florida Farmworker Housing Initiative
programs.who: Agricultural workers and their families across rural Florida
programs.what: Safe housing near work sites - often the most underserved population in Florida. Many units available in Immokalee, Belle Glade.

programs.name: Sadowski Housing Trust Fund – Elderly
programs.who: Florida seniors 62+ earning below 80% AMI
programs.what: Funds senior housing construction and subsidised rent in senior-designated communities. Wait lists vary by county.

programs.name: FL Veterans Housing Assistance – VASH + State Grant
programs.org: Florida Department of Veterans' Affairs + HUD
programs.who: Florida veterans who are homeless or at risk
programs.what: Combines HUD-VASH vouchers with Florida's own veterans housing grant - covers rent, deposits, and case management.

rights.text: Security deposit must be returned within 30 days of lease termination with itemised deductions.
Landlord must keep the premises in good repair and fit for habitation.
Landlord must give reasonable advance notice before entering - courts apply a reasonableness standard.
Tenant may terminate the lease if landlord fails to keep property habitable after written notice.
A landlord must give 60 days' notice (tenant 30 days) to end a month-to-month tenancy.

State law requires landlords to give tenants specific written disclosures (for example, the owner or manager's identity, how the security deposit is held, shared-utility arrangements, and known hazards).

A landlord cannot retaliate against a tenant (for example by raising rent, cutting services, or evicting) for complaining about conditions or exercising a legal right. Retaliation is presumed if the landlord takes a negative action within 3 months of the tenant's protected act.
Eviction for unpaid rent: the landlord may demand rent when due; the tenant then has 7 days to pay to avoid eviction.
Georgia courts recognize a tenant's right to repair and deduct when the landlord won't make major repairs.

programs.name: Georgia Department of Community Affairs – Rental Assistance
programs.who: Low-income Georgia renters, households in crisis
programs.what: Administers Section 8 vouchers, emergency rental assistance, and affordable housing development across Georgia.

programs.name: Georgia Dream Homeownership Programme
programs.who: First-time homebuyers below income limits; renters ready to transition
programs.what: Down payment assistance of $7,500–$10,000 for qualifying buyers, including public servants, healthcare workers, and educators.

programs.name: HUD-VASH Georgia
programs.who: Georgia veterans experiencing homelessness
programs.what: Rental vouchers combined with case management at VA facilities. Atlanta and Savannah have the largest programs.

programs.name: Georgia NAACP Fair Housing Legal Assistance
programs.who: Black and minority renters facing discrimination or eviction
programs.what: Free legal representation in eviction cases, housing discrimination complaints, and lease disputes. Call 404-524-5286.

programs.name: APS (Atlanta Public Schools) Family Stability Housing
programs.who: Families with children enrolled in Atlanta public schools facing homelessness
programs.what: Emergency housing stability funding ensuring school-age children maintain stable housing and school enrollment.

rights.text: Security deposit must be returned within 14 days of move-out with an itemised statement of deductions.
Landlord must give at least 2 days' notice before non-emergency entry.
Landlord must maintain the premises in a habitable condition and make repairs within a reasonable time.
Tenant may repair and deduct costs if landlord fails to maintain essential services after proper notice.
A landlord must give 45 days' notice (tenant 28 days) to end a month-to-month tenancy.
A landlord may charge a security deposit of no more than 1 month's rent.
A late charge cannot exceed 8% of the rent due.

State law requires landlords to give tenants specific written disclosures (for example, the owner or manager's identity, how the security deposit is held, shared-utility arrangements, and known hazards).
A landlord cannot retaliate against a tenant (for example by raising rent, cutting services, or evicting) for complaining about conditions or exercising a legal right.
Eviction for unpaid rent: the tenant has 5 days to pay or move.
If the landlord fails to make major repairs, the tenant may withhold rent or repair and deduct the cost under state law.

programs.name: HHFDC Rental Housing Programme
programs.org: Hawaii Housing Finance and Development Corporation
programs.who: Low-to-moderate income Hawaii renters across all islands
programs.what: Finances affordable rental housing and administers Section 8 vouchers. Hawaii's extremely high rents make this critical.

programs.name: Native Hawaiian Housing Block Grant
programs.who: Native Hawaiians (50%+ Hawaiian ancestry) on or near homestead lands
programs.what: Low-cost leases on Hawaiian homestead land; also rental assistance and homeownership support for Native Hawaiians.

programs.name: UH System Student Emergency Housing Fund
programs.who: UH students experiencing housing insecurity
programs.what: Emergency grants for rent and deposits; connections to affordable off-campus housing near each UH campus.

programs.name: Hawaii Kupuna Care – Senior Housing
programs.who: Hawaii seniors 60+, especially Native Hawaiian elders
programs.what: Subsidised senior housing and home repair assistance to help kupuna (elders) age in place in their communities.

programs.name: Section 8 Moderate Rehabilitation (Mod Rehab)
programs.who: Very low-income island residents
programs.what: Project-based rental assistance in rehabilitated housing stock - particularly important on neighbour islands with limited stock.

rights.text: Security deposit must be returned within 21 days of move-out with an itemised deduction list.
Landlord must give 24 hours' notice before entering for non-emergency maintenance.
Landlord must maintain the dwelling in a habitable condition and make timely repairs.
Tenant may withhold rent for landlord's failure to maintain habitability after proper written notice.
To end a month-to-month tenancy, either party must give one month's notice.
Courts recognize retaliation as a defense: a landlord cannot evict or penalize a tenant for complaining about conditions or exercising a legal right.
Eviction for unpaid rent: the tenant has 3 days to pay or move.
Idaho tenants can sue to force repairs; repair-and-deduct is limited to smoke detectors.

programs.name: Idaho Housing and Finance Association – Rental Assistance
programs.who: Low-income Idaho renters, working families, seniors
programs.what: Administers Section 8, HOME-funded units, and the Rental Assistance Programme. Apply at IdahoHousing.org.

programs.name: Idaho Migrant & Seasonal Farmworker Housing
programs.who: Agricultural workers and their families - primarily Hispanic/Latino
programs.what: Safe housing near work sites in the Magic Valley and Snake River Plain areas where Idaho agriculture is concentrated.

programs.name: IHFA Senior Affordable Housing
programs.who: Idaho seniors 62+ earning below 60% AMI
programs.what: LIHTC-financed senior apartment communities across Idaho with income-based rents.

programs.name: Idaho Veterans Services Housing Support
programs.org: Idaho Division of Veterans Services + VA Boise
programs.who: Idaho veterans facing homelessness or housing instability
programs.what: Emergency housing grants, HUD-VASH vouchers, and transitional housing through the Boise and Idaho Falls VA.

programs.name: Community Action Partnership – Idaho Emergency Assistance
programs.who: Low-income Idaho residents in crisis, including indigenous communities
programs.what: Emergency rent, utility, and deposit assistance. Find your local CAP agency at IdahoCommunityAction.org.

rights.text: Deposit must be returned within 30 days of move-out (45 days if deductions are claimed).
Chicago landlords must give at least 2 days' notice before entering a unit.
If landlord fails to repair within 14 days of notice, tenant may withhold rent or repair and deduct.
Landlord must pay interest on security deposits held for more than 6 months (Chicago).
To end a month-to-month tenancy, either party must give 30 days' notice.

State law requires landlords to give tenants specific written disclosures (for example, the owner or manager's identity, how the security deposit is held, shared-utility arrangements, and known hazards).
A landlord cannot retaliate against a tenant (for example by raising rent, cutting services, or evicting) for complaining about conditions or exercising a legal right.
Eviction for unpaid rent: the tenant has 5 days to pay or move.
If the landlord fails to make major repairs, the tenant may repair the problem and deduct the cost from rent under state law.

programs.name: IHDA Rental Housing Support Programme
programs.who: Very low-income Illinois renters, below 30% AMI
programs.what: Pays the difference between market rent and 30% of income for the lowest-income renters statewide.

programs.name: Chicago ERAP / Renters Assistance Fund
programs.who: Chicago renters behind on rent or utilities
programs.what: Emergency rental assistance up to 18 months; priority for households with children, seniors, and disabled individuals.

programs.name: Woodstock Institute – Fair Housing for Minority Renters
programs.who: Black, Latino, and immigrant renters in Chicago metro
programs.what: Legal aid, fair housing testing, and anti-displacement advocacy in Chicago's historically redlined neighborhoods.

programs.name: Illinois Student Assistance Commission – Housing Help
programs.who: Illinois college students with housing insecurity
programs.what: Emergency financial grants covering rent and basic needs; each state-funded institution also has a Basic Needs centre.

programs.name: Illinois Veterans Supportive Living Programme
programs.who: Illinois veterans - especially those with service-connected disabilities
programs.what: Supportive housing with on-site services across Illinois; also rapid rehousing for veterans exiting homelessness.

rights.text: Security deposit must be returned within 45 days of move-out with an itemised deduction statement.
Landlord must give reasonable notice before entering - statute does not specify hours but practice is 24 hours.
Landlord must maintain the unit in a habitable condition compliant with health and safety codes.
Tenant may terminate lease for uninhabitable conditions after giving the landlord reasonable notice to cure.
To end a month-to-month tenancy, either party must give one month's notice.

State law requires landlords to give tenants specific written disclosures (for example, the owner or manager's identity, how the security deposit is held, shared-utility arrangements, and known hazards).
A landlord cannot retaliate against a tenant (for example by raising rent, cutting services, or evicting) for complaining about conditions or exercising a legal right.
Eviction for unpaid rent: the tenant has 10 days to pay or move.
Indiana tenants can sue to enforce repairs; there is no statutory rent withholding or repair-and-deduct.

programs.name: IHCDA Section 8 & Rental Assistance
programs.org: Indiana Housing and Community Development Authority
programs.who: Low-income Indiana renters, families, elderly, disabled
programs.what: Section 8 vouchers administered through local housing authorities; search for available units at IndianaHousing.org.

programs.name: Indiana Emergency Rental Assistance (IERA)
programs.who: Indiana renters at risk of eviction due to COVID or economic hardship
programs.what: Covers overdue rent and utilities; apply through INRentHelp.com.

programs.name: Indiana NAACP Fair Housing – Black Renter Assistance
programs.who: Black Indiana renters facing discrimination, eviction, or predatory leases

rights.text: Security deposit must be returned within 30 days of move-out with an itemised deduction statement.
Landlord must give 24 hours' notice before entering for non-emergency inspections or repairs.
Landlord must maintain the premises in a fit and habitable condition and make repairs promptly.
Tenant may repair and deduct up to $500 or ½ month's rent (whichever is less) if landlord fails to act.
To end a month-to-month tenancy, either party must give 30 days' notice.
A landlord may charge a security deposit of no more than 2 months' rent.
Late fees are capped: for rent of $700 or less, $12/day up to $60/month; for rent over $700, $20/day up to $100/month.

State law requires landlords to give tenants specific written disclosures (for example, the owner or manager's identity, how the security deposit is held, shared-utility arrangements, and known hazards).

A landlord cannot retaliate against a tenant (for example by raising rent, cutting services, or evicting) for complaining about conditions or exercising a legal right. Retaliation is presumed if the landlord takes a negative action within 1 year of the tenant's protected act.
Eviction for unpaid rent: the tenant has 3 days to pay or move.
If the landlord fails to make major repairs, the tenant may withhold rent or repair and deduct the cost under state law.

rights.text: Security deposit must be returned within 30 days of lease end with an itemised deduction statement.
Landlord must maintain the premises in a safe and healthy condition complying with applicable codes.
Landlord must give reasonable advance notice - typically 24 hours - before non-emergency entry.
Tenant may withhold rent for landlord's failure to maintain habitability after written notice.
A landlord may end a month-to-month tenancy only for just cause, with 30 days' notice.
A landlord may charge a security deposit of no more than 1 month's rent or $100, whichever is greater.
A landlord cannot demand an amount greater than the whole rent owed when rent is late.

State law requires landlords to give tenants specific written disclosures (for example, the owner or manager's identity, how the security deposit is held, shared-utility arrangements, and known hazards).

A landlord cannot retaliate against a tenant (for example by raising rent, cutting services, or evicting) for complaining about conditions or exercising a legal right. Retaliation is presumed if the landlord takes a negative action within 6 months of the tenant's protected act.
Eviction for unpaid rent: the tenant has 7 days to pay or move.
If the landlord fails to make major repairs, the tenant may withhold rent under state law.

programs.name: American Samoa Housing Authority (ASHA)
programs.who: Low-income American Samoa residents - AS has limited land and high costs for construction materials shipped from the mainland
programs.what: Public housing and limited Section 8 vouchers for qualifying American Samoa residents.

programs.name: Samoan Extended Family (Aiga) Housing System
programs.who: Samoan residents following fa'asamoa (Samoan way) - extended family housing is the primary social safety net
programs.what: While not a government program, understanding that traditional aiga networks provide housing support for community members in need is essential.

programs.name: FEMA Disaster Housing Assistance – American Samoa
programs.who: AS residents displaced by cyclones, tsunamis, or other disasters
programs.what: Federal disaster housing assistance for US nationals in American Samoa during and after declared disasters.

programs.name: ASCC (American Samoa Community College) Student Housing
programs.who: ASCC students from outer islands or remote villages
programs.what: Limited on-campus housing and emergency housing assistance for students experiencing instability at American Samoa's only community college.

programs.name: AS Veterans Assistance (National Guard & Reservists)
programs.who: American Samoa veterans - AS has the highest per-capita military enlistment rate of any US territory
programs.what: Housing assistance referrals and connection to mainland VA services; AS veterans often face unique challenges accessing mainland benefits.

rights.text: Security deposit must be returned within a reasonable time after move-out with itemised deductions.
Landlord must keep the premises in a safe and habitable condition throughout the tenancy.
Landlord must give reasonable advance notice before entering the unit for non-emergency purposes.
Eviction proceedings must follow formal court process under CNMI Superior Court rules.

programs.name: CNMI Housing Authority Public Housing & Section 8
programs.org: Commonwealth of the Northern Mariana Islands Housing Authority
programs.who: Low-income CNMI residents - the CNMI has extremely limited affordable housing stock
programs.what: Limited public housing and Section 8 vouchers for qualifying CNMI residents on Saipan, Tinian, and Rota.

programs.name: CHamoru & Carolinian Indigenous Housing Rights
programs.who: Indigenous CHamoru and Carolinian residents of the CNMI - the indigenous peoples have special land rights
programs.what: Indigenous land rights and housing protections under CNMI law; community-based housing support for indigenous residents.

programs.name: FEMA Disaster Housing – CNMI (Super Typhoon Recovery)
programs.who: CNMI residents displaced by super typhoons - the CNMI is in one of the most typhoon-active regions on earth
programs.what: Federal disaster housing assistance during and after catastrophic typhoons; the CNMI experienced major destruction from Typhoon Yutu in 2018.

programs.name: NMC (Northern Marianas College) Student Housing
programs.who: NMC students from outer islands of Tinian and Rota
programs.what: On-campus and emergency housing assistance for students at the CNMI's main higher education institution.

programs.name: CNMI Veterans Housing Assistance
programs.who: CNMI veterans and National Guard members
programs.what: Housing referrals and connection to mainland VA services; CNMI veterans may access services through the VA Pacific Islands Health Care System.
